mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate suppresses the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces corporate debt service burdens, which mechanically increases present values and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day S&P 500 forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,150 thousand units signal robust residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, durable goods, and credit, which lifts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1,150.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated levels of industrial production and retail sales indicate a high-throughput equilibrium where supply capacity is fully utilized to meet consumer demand, reducing inventory volatility and boosting earnings predictability, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint-signal portfolio fails to outperform the single-signal portfolios.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of payroll employment during zero-lower-bound monetary regimes triggers market expectations of premature policy normalization and tighter financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of PAYEMS expansion months during ZLB periods, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105 signals a contraction in goods-producing sector activity that precedes downward earnings revisions and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate crossing above 0.5% marks the exit from extreme monetary accommodation, raising short-term discount rates and money-market yields that divert capital from equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency is below 55% when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, or the mean 5-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a low unemployment rate with a low federal funds rate generates a Goldilocks labor market where employment income supports consumption without triggering wage-push inflation or aggressive policy tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-FEDFUNDS interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of real GDP reflects a large aggregate output base that sustains corporate revenue growth through thick-market externalities and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 levels and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
